channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Missouri man Travis Timmerman, missing since June, freed from Syrian prison
item.description: A Missouri man found in Syria said early Friday he was freed following the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad after seven months in prison.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Hammer, ABC 4

item.title: 2 cats die from suspected bird flu traced to raw milk in California
item.description: Health officials are investigating possible cases of H5 bird flu after two cats died from consuming recalled raw milk, according to the Los Angeles County Department of Public Health.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Bird strike disables jetliner engine, forces emergency landing at JFK airport
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A bird strike involving an American Airlines jetliner disabled one of the plane's two engines shortly after takeoff from New York's LaGuardia Airport, forcing the flight to turn around and land at John F. Kennedy International Airport, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EU nations agree a crackdown on migrant smugglers. Some fear it may target people trying to help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union countries agreed on Friday a draft law aimed at preventing and countering migrant smuggling, which critics say could be used to target people or charity groups that try to help migrants in trouble.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thousands of Syrians celebrate in central Damascus during first Friday prayers since Assad's fall
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Thousands of Syrians gathered Friday at Umayyad Square, the largest in Damascus, to celebrate after the first Muslim Friday prayers following the downfall of former President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Amazon to donate $1 million to Trump inauguration, matching Meta
item.description: E-commerce giant Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump’s inaugural fund, matching the amount Meta already donated to the incoming president’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: Ivy League murder suspect acted superior, did not expect to be caught: body language expert
item.description: Body language expert Susan Constantine analyzed videos and photos of Luigi Mangione, who is accused of shooting UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson dead on Dec. 4.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida woman arrested after threatening Blue Cross Blue Shield following denied medical claims: police
item.description: A Florida woman is behind bars after allegedly threatening Blue Cross Blue Shield by saying, "Deny, delay, depose. You people are next," following recently denied medical claims.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: O’Malley: Calling Social Security a 'legal Ponzi scheme' is bull
item.description: "Social Security is not on a path for bankruptcy or insolvency," O'Malley said during a Thursday appearance on "The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and is pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York City mayor meets with Trump's 'border czar' to discuss how to go after 'violent' criminals
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams met with President-elect Donald Trump's incoming “border czar” on Thursday, with the Democratic mayor expressing an enthusiasm to work with the incoming administration to pursue violent criminals in the city while Trump promises mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk wants to turn SpaceX's Starbase site into a Texas city
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk 's company on Thursday sent a letter to local officials requesting an election to turn what it calls Starbase — the South Texas site where SpaceX builds and launches its massive Starship rockets — into an incorporated city.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump extends unprecedented invites to China's Xi and other world leaders for his inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has invited Chinese President Xi Jinping and other world leaders to his inauguration next month.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Proposed 'Trump' law seeks to allow felons to run for office in Missouri
item.description: A Missouri representative is proposing a repeal of a state law that currently prevents citizens with a felony history from running for a state office.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton said he is open to having a conversation with President Joe Biden about a potential preemptive pardon for his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump again named Time's Person of the Year
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man arrested on DC Metrobus for fare evasion found carrying loaded shotgun: police
item.description: Gerald Evans, 30, allegedly had a loaded shotgun on him when Metro Transit Police arrested him on a Washington, D.C., Metrobus for not complying when issued a citation for fare evasion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dick Van Dyke, Cher among those forced to flee Malibu homes in wake of wildfire
item.description: Officials said the fire, which grew significantly overnight, was only 7% contained as of Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect intends to plead not guilty: lawyer
item.description: Alleged UnitedHealthcare CEO shooter Luigi Mangione was arrested at a Pennsylvania McDonald's on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Kylie Kelce dethrones Joe Rogan on podcast charts as her debut episode hits No. 1
item.description: Kylie Kelce, wife of former NFL player turned media personality Jason Kelce, beat out Joe Rogan for the top podcast spot on both Apple and Spotify this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: DA suggests unusual idea for halting Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Eager to preserve President-elect Donald Trump ’s hush money conviction even as he returns to office, prosecutors are suggesting various ways forward — including one based on how some courts handle criminal cases when defendants die. Trump’s spokesperson called the ideas “pathetic.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When fire threatened a California university, the school says it knew what to do
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — The wildfire alert came in the middle of the night as some college students in Southern California were cramming for final exams and others were woken up in their dorms.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN and ERIC THAYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mangione family in Maryland: What we know
item.description: Although many people outside of Maryland came to know the "Mangione" name only after the arrest of Luigi Mangione, 26, who is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, the family has a number of prominent ties to the state where he grew up.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, Fox 5

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Geminids meteor shower peaks this weekend: How to watch
item.description: People across the globe could see up to 120 meteors per hour during the peak of the Geminids meteor shower this week.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, Fox 5

item.title: Greene says she is safe after bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) confirmed she is safe after reports swirled of an email sent to law enforcement containing a bomb threat targeting the lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell receives medical attention after falling
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) received medical attention after falling following a Republican lunch meeting Tuesday. A spokesman for McConnell said the GOP leader tripped after the lunch. "He sustained a minor cut to the face and sprained his wrist.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man is charged with burning American flag during Washington protest over Netanyahu visit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal authorities have charged a man with burning an American flag in the nation’s capital during protests against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s address to Congress in July. Michael Snow Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Details emerge about suspect charged with murder in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: ALTOONA, Pa. (AP) — The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO likely was motivated by his anger with what he called “parasitic” health insurance companies and a disdain for corporate greed, a law enforcement bulletin obtained by The Associated Press said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US officials to investigate labor and human rights abuses in Nicaragua
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration is opening an investigation into labor and human rights abuses in Nicaragua, impacting relations with a country the U.S. has a free trade deal with amid growing concerns over President Daniel Ortega’s increasingly authoritarian rule.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, Fox 5

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows thieves speed off in sports cars from Michigan dealership
item.description: A group of suspected car thieves sped away from a Michigan dealership in rare sports vehicles, leading police on a high-speed chase that ended with the arrest of a 19-year-old Detroit woman.
item.dc:creator: Duncan MacLean, WGN 9
